key_strengths: World's only vertically integrated eyewear company with ~50% global lens market share and dominant brands (Ray-Ban, Oakley, Varilux)
Non-discretionary demand base — half the world needs vision correction, creating highly recurring revenue
Record FCF of $3.8B with 163% conversion of net income; tangible ROIC of 22-33% hidden behind acquisition goodwill
Conservative balance sheet: 1.1x net debt/EBITDA, 14.4x interest coverage, €5.7B liquidity
Emerging AI smart glasses platform (7M+ units in FY2025) with decade-long Meta partnership
key_risks: Smart glasses platform competition from Apple, Google, Samsung could commoditize EL's manufacturing role
US tariff exposure material (43% of revenue) with 20-145% tariff rates on imports
Three-year Delfin governance crisis unresolved; CEO dual-hatting creates structural conflicts
Margin recovery from 16% to 19-20% adjusted operating margin not yet demonstrated
Gross margin declined 280bps YoY (63.4% to 60.6%) — largest drop in recent history
red_flags: Supreme acquisition ($1.5B) represents strategic drift from core eyewear competency
IFRS-to-adjusted operating margin gap of 410 basis points requires scrutiny
Working capital turned negative (-€144M) for first time in data series
Reported ROIC of ~5% below estimated WACC of ~9% on reported capital base
buy_triggers: FY2026 Q1/Q2 results confirm margin progression toward 19-20% adjusted operating margin target
US tariff de-escalation or successful mitigation evidenced by stable/improving US segment margins
Stock declines below €180 without fundamental deterioration, creating deep value entry
sell_triggers: Apple launches smart glasses AND vertically integrates its own lens manufacturing
FY2026 adjusted operating margin comes in below 17%, suggesting structural margin impairment
Meta terminates or materially downgrades the smart glasses partnership
valuation_summary: At €204.70 (23.7x forward earnings), the market prices in only 4-5% annual earnings growth — well below historical 8-10% trend. Base-case discounted intrinsic value of ~€265 implies 30% upside; bear-case €162 implies 21% downside. Owner earnings of ~€7.10/share put the stock at 29x owner earnings.
source_summary: EssilorLuxottica FY2025 press release (Feb 2026), yfinance auto-fetched financials, Moody's credit opinion (Sep 2025), Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, GuruFocus, Bloomberg, CNBC, analyst consensus (22 analysts), company investor relations
change_notes: Initial analysis. Stock near 52-week low after 37% drawdown driven by tariff escalation, smart glasses competition fears, and FY2025 margin disappointment.
